channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Your ‘new’ Seagate data center hard drive is likely a used one
item.description: A growing number of buyers have reported purchasing supposedly new Seagate data center-grade hard drives, only to discover that they had been previously used for thousands of hours.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Ignore that Nvidia RTX 5060 rumor claiming 16GB VRAM – it’s fake, and I’m still worried about how the purported 8GB GPU will perform
item.description: RTX 5060 rumor of 16GB of video RAM was based on a faked video, sadly – the grapevine insists the GPU will stick with just 8GB.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: JD Vance Makes Debut Abroad as US Vice President at AI Summit in France
item.description: US Vice President JD Vance makes his debut abroad at an artificial intelligence summit in Paris, pushing back on European censorship and promoting free speech.
item.dc:creator: Sylvie Corbet And Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: How Elon Musk is using tech to DOGE the government
item.description: Elon Musk is using his tech and leadership playbook in a crusade against the federal bureaucracy as he and his allies attempt to gain access to sensitive information and use emerging tech to speed up the process.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Changing a few lines of code in Linux could apparently save hyperscalers billions, research claims, but I am not convinced
item.description: Research suggests changing a few lines of code in the Linux kernel can save hyperscalers billions by cutting data center energy consumption.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: How the slash-and-burn tactics Musk brought to Washington backfired at Twitter
item.description: When Elon Musk bought Twitter in 2022, he laid off thousands of employees, stopped paying rent and auctioned off coffee makers and office chairs in hopes of a big turnaround.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BERNARD CONDON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Amazon to pay nearly $4M to settle lawsuit alleging it took tips from drivers
item.description: Amazon has agreed to pay nearly $4 million to settle charges that the e-commerce company subsidized its labor costs by taking tips its delivery drivers received from customers.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: How AI could replace government workers to reduce spending
item.description: Elon Musk and DOGE are reportedly using artificial intelligence to analyze federal spending and where cuts an be made
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: AWS plans to outspend Microsoft and Google on AI infrastructure
item.description: Amazon Web Services (AWS) intends to invest $100 billion in ramping up infrastructure for AI cloud services this year, surpassing the spending plans of rivals Microsoft and Google.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: New law in Brazil is making students put away their smartphones at school
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Brazilian students returned to class this week with a new task: staying away from their smartphones as a new law restricting their use in schools took effect.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: GSA plans moving staff to Interior headquarters, as its tech shop anticipates more shakeups
item.description: GSA senior leaders are saying the Trump administration’s long-term goal is to merge GSA, OPM and OMB into a single agency. The post GSA plans moving staff to Interior headquarters, as its tech shop anticipates more shakeups first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: Zuckerberg visits White House to discuss advancing 'American tech leadership abroad': Meta
item.description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg visited the White House on Thursday to discuss Meta's role in helping the Trump administration with America's international tech leadership, the company confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Nebraska government system shutdown disrupts the Legislature, DMV and other offices
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — A systemwide outage of state goverment computers shut down everything from the work of the Legislature to driver’s licensing offices for several hours Thursday.
item.dc:creator: MARGERY A. BECK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Google rolls out cheaper AI model as industry scrutinizes costs
item.description: Google has announced several updates to its Gemini portfolio, including a budget-friendly product, amid growing demand for low-cost AI models driven by the rise of Chinese competitor DeepSeek.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: One Tech Tip: How to block your phone from tracking your location
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Smartphones are useful tools for everyday life, but they're privy to nearly everything about you, including all the places you've been — if you let them.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Malicious package found in the Go ecosystem – update
item.description: Update February 6: Google has removed the package and provided the following statement: The module has been removed from both the Go module proxy and GitHub, and we’ve added it to the Go vulnerability database for anyone who thinks they may have been impacted.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: AI takes big step forward with 3D-printed shoe
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fast fashion, laptops and toys are likely to cost more due to US tariffs on Chinese imports
item.description: A sweeping new U.S. tariff on products made in China is expected to increase the prices American consumers pay for a wide array of products, from the ultra-cheap apparel sold on online shopping platforms to toys and electronic devices such as computers and cellphones.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: China targets Apple’s business practices; maybe the company should adapt
item.description: They say great art reflects its time. When it comes to Apple’s business, just as the iMac and iPod matched the zeitgeist, the company must now develop its business to meet the complex demands of a divided, post-globalist world struggling with economic and climate insecurity.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Hands-on with the Windows answer to Apple’s Vision Pro
item.description: Hey, remember the Apple Vision Pro? For a while there, everyone was talking about Apple’s $3,499 headset being the future of productivity. You’d strap a headset to your face and work on large monitors floating in a “mixed reality” world.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How to bring Google’s custom vibration brilliance to any Android phone today
item.description: Have you heard the buzz? Good vibrations are on their way to Android — or at least to Google’s own homemade Pixel devices.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Apple lashes out at iPhone porn app maker and the EU rules allowing its download
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Apple has chided a newly introduced pornography app available in the European Union, warning that the bloc’s digital rules opening the way for third-party app store downloads undermines consumer confidence in the tech giant.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge says Elon Musk's claims of harm from OpenAI are a 'stretch' but welcomes possible trial
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (AP) — Elon Musk's lawyers faced off with OpenAI in court Tuesday as a federal judge weighed the billionaire's request for a court order that would block the ChatGPT maker from converting itself to a for-profit company. U.S.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY and MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Cisco: Latest news and insights
item.description: Cisco is the dominant vendor in enterprise networking, and under CEO Chuck Robbins, it continues to shake things up. Its most recent mega-acquisition is its $28 billion purchase of Splunk, a bold move that adds revenue opportunities in observability, data analytics, AIOps, and genAI.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Google makes its appeal to overturn jury verdict branding the Play Store as illegal monopoly
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google went to appeals court Monday in an attempt to convince a three-judge panel to overturn a jury's verdict declaring its app store for Android smartphones as an illegal monopoly and block the penalties imposed by a federal judge to stop the misbehavior.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Bill Gates shares his thoughts on vaccine backlash, Intel's woes and Google's antitrust battle
item.description: Although Microsoft founder Bill Gates sat down for an interview with The Associated Press to discuss his new memoir, “Source Code: My Beginnings,” he also shared his views on a variety of other topics, including vaccine conspiracy views about him, his thoughts on the struggles of longtime computer c
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, ABC 4
